t_ch: 愿你…活在永恒的痛苦中…！
t_en: I curse you... to live in eternal pain...!

t_ch: 求您…赐我解脱……
t_en: Please... grant me release...

t_ch: 你知道该怎么做。
t_en: You know what to do.

t_ch: 这一切…还要持续到什么时候…？
t_en: When will this ever end...?

t_ch: 来吧，完成你的使命。
t_en: Come. Fulfill your duty.

t_ch: 什么……
t_en: ...What?

t_ch: 呃…怎么是大地兽？
t_en: Uh... Why is there a dromas here?

t_ch: （我的神血蜜露…？！）
t_en: (Where did my Dew of Divine Blood go...!?)

t_ch: 真是性格顽劣…阁下，跟我来！
t_en: Ugh, she's incorrigible... Follow me!

t_ch: …就是这里了。
t_en: ...Here we are.

t_ch: 这是…遐蝶的礼物？
t_en: This is Castorice's gift...?

t_ch: 妈妈说让我们先走，可她一直没有过来。
t_en: Mommy told us to go ahead first, but she's still not here yet.

t_ch: 一，二，三…已经数不过来天数了。
t_en: One, two, three... I don't even know how many days have passed since then.

t_ch: 也许数完十根手指，妈妈就回来了。
t_en: Perhaps Mommy will be back once you finish counting the days on all ten fingers.

t_ch: 一，二，三…早就超过十根手指的天数了。
t_en: One, two, three... It's been much longer than ten days since we left Mommy.

t_ch: 好漂亮的蝴蝶，是在和我们说话吗？
t_en: What a beautiful butterfly. Is it talking to us?

t_ch: 这是「亡语蝶」，死难者众多的地方，总有它们翩飞的身影。
t_en: This is a Eulogistic Butterfly. They are found in places where many have met their demise.

t_ch: 在哀地里亚，有些人认为它们是{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}的仁慈。它将死者的最后一道执念，化作这些生灵，就像是为生命制作了标本。
t_en: In Aidonia, some think of them as manifestations of {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#}' benevolence. By turning the lingering obsessions of the deceased into these creatures, it is as if the Titan has made life into a specimen.

t_ch: 也有另一些人认为，它们本就是{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}的信使，引渡亡魂去往冥界。
t_en: Others think of them as {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#}' messengers that guide the departed to the nether realm.

t_ch: 感觉后一种说法更可信呢，毕竟它的发言还是有点太…嗯，直抒胸臆？
t_en: The second theory sounds more believable. After all, the things they're saying are rather... um, direct?

t_ch: 或许吧。但能听见它们窃窃私语，至少说明我们确实在向死者的世界靠近。
t_en: Perhaps. But the fact that we're able to hear their whispers proves that we're fast approaching the realm of the dead.

t_ch: 可是，师父…那位姐姐，难道骗了我们？
t_en: But master... Did that lady really lie to us?

t_ch: 百夫长亲自嘱咐过，要把那个铭刻镶嵌在他的盾牌上。
t_en: The centurion personally instructed us to inlay the inscription into his shield.

t_ch: 可现在，却被她拿走了……
t_en: But now she's taken it away...

t_ch: 孩子啊…盾牌永远不可能铸成，也不需要了……
t_en: My child... that shield can never be cast, and there's no need for it anymore...

t_ch: 来，孩子，百夫长叫什么名字？…不，忘了我刚才的话吧。
t_en: Come, child, what is the name of that centurion again? Actually... Never mind, forget I said that.

t_ch: 我陪你一起等她回来。那时候，你就可以继续打造盾牌了。
t_en: I'll wait with you for her to come back. When that happens, you'll be able to keep making shields.

t_ch: 你们…要买鱼吗？
t_en: Do you... want to buy any fish?

t_ch: 咦？他能看见我们？
t_en: Huh? He can see us?

t_ch: 怎么…又是活着的人？
t_en: Wait... More living people?

t_ch: 活着的人…不卖，你们…太无耻了。买了鱼却不付账…等我再见到她，我非得…我非得……
t_en: I'm not selling my fish to... living people. You lot are too... shameless. Buying fish without paying... If I ever see her again, I will... I will...

t_ch: 难道说，你们…是未能安息的灵魂？
t_en: Could it be that... You're all dead souls that never achieved rest?

t_ch: 冥界…子虚乌有……
t_en: The nether realm... Does not exist...

t_ch: 泰坦…拒绝…你…我……
t_en: Titan... Rejects... You... Me...

t_ch: 所以…要买鱼吗？
t_en: So... do you want to buy any fish?

t_ch: 可是，附近也没有「鱼」呀……
t_en: But there aren't any "fish" nearby...

t_ch: 鱼…不见了？人…也不见了……
t_en: Are the fish... gone? The people... are gone, too...

t_ch: 只能…卖鱼。你…要买鱼吗？
t_en: I can only... keep selling fish. So... do you... want to buy any fish?

t_ch: 对不起，我的积蓄所剩无多。但或许有别的办法，能解脱你的痛苦。
t_en: Apologies, I don't have much in the way of savings, but perhaps there's another way for me to release you from your suffering.

t_ch: 请放松。愿你在温柔的花乡中…平静睡去。
t_en: Please relax. May you fall asleep peacefully... amid a gentle field of flowers.

t_ch: 这…这是……
t_en: This... this...

t_ch: 果然，这些人不是幻影，而是死难者的魂灵。
t_en: I knew it. These people aren't illusions. They're the souls of the deceased.

t_ch: 他们本该顺着冥河，去往西风的尽头才对…如今却搁浅在了冥流的浅滩上，徘徊不前。
t_en: They're supposed to reach the end of the west wind by floating down the River of Souls... Yet they have floundered upon the shoals of the stream, unable to move forward.

t_ch: 是{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}拒绝了他们？还是他们根本没有真正死去？
t_en: Did {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#} reject them? Or perhaps they didn't really die at all?

t_ch: 是和我类似的情况？
t_en: Are they in the same situation as me?

t_ch: 也许我们都还能抢救一下。
t_en: Perhaps all of us can still use some CPR.

t_ch: 你怎么现在就以死者自居啦，别放弃呀！
t_en: Why are you identifying as a dead person now? Don't give up on yourself!

t_ch: 我不知道，这情景…闻所未闻。
t_en: I have no idea. This circumstance... is completely unprecedented.

t_ch: 等等……
t_en: Hang on...

t_ch: 他刚才是不是说了…活着的人？他见过的生者，不仅仅只有我们？
t_en: Didn't he say... "more living people"? So that means he's seen other members of the living apart from us?

t_ch: 莫非，亡魂滞留于此，是受到了某个人的诓骗？
t_en: Could it be that these souls are stuck here due to someone's deceit?

t_ch: 若是出于好心，还算得上「骗」嘛？
t_en: If something is done out of goodwill, are you really gonna call it "deceit"?

t_ch: 好心？这无论怎么看都……
t_en: Goodwill? I can't see anything good out of—

t_ch: 等等…赛飞儿阁下？
t_en: Wait a minute... Lady Cipher?

t_ch: 没错，就是我。
t_en: That's me.

t_ch: 你怎么在这？
t_en: What are you doing here?

t_ch: 你也死了吗？
t_en: Are you dead too?

t_ch: 呃…暂时还和你「阴阳两隔」着呢！
t_en: Umm... I still belong to the land of the living as of now, thank you very much!

t_ch: 亡魂们之前遇见的生者，莫非就是……
t_en: Could it be that... You are the living being that those souls met...?

t_ch: 实在看不过眼，所以东边偷了点，西边骗了点，希望给他们的生活多添一点盼头。
t_en: I couldn't take their behavior anymore, so I stole some things here and made up some lies there... Just trying to give them something to look forward to, you know?

t_ch: …就是因为有像你这样恶劣的生者，我们才会被大家误会！
t_en: ...It's because of vile living creatures like you that we're being misunderstood by all of them!

t_ch: 不然呢，这地头什么光景你们也看见了——这些可怜人被冥界拒之门外，困在自己生前的几个弹指里面，进退不得。
t_en: What else am I supposed do? You can see the mess this place is in — These poor souls are denied by the nether realm, so now they're stuck in a few brief moments of their life, unable to move forward or back.

t_ch: 路上见过两个铁匠没？不知道他们在那儿呆了多久，一遍又一遍地想锻出面盾牌来…噫，斯缇科西亚，太可怕啦。
t_en: Did you see those two blacksmiths on the way? They've been here for as long as the sun's been around, wailing over and over about casting a shield... Styxia is so freaky.

t_ch: 所以，赛飞儿阁下为何又回来了？
t_en: Then why have you come back, Lady Cipher?

t_ch: 别误会，我可不是来帮你们探险的…毕竟，我这辈子最讨厌的就是水了，何况还是这臭烘烘的冥河……
t_en: Hey, I'm not here to aid your adventure. I hate water more than anything else in this world, especially that stinky River of Souls...

t_ch: …咳，我只是临走前突然有些发现，觉得高低该给你们递个话。
t_en: But... I just discovered some stuff before I left, and I reckon you should know about it.

t_ch: 那个拒绝死亡的孩子——我在这里发现了他的足迹哪？
t_en: It's about that boy who defied death... I found traces of him here.

t_ch: 万敌…阁下？
t_en: Lord Mydei...?

t_ch: 想起来了，是叫这个名！不管怎么说，话我已经带到了…至于有没有用，你们自己定夺吧。
t_en: Oh right! That's his name! Well, now you know. As for whether that info is actually useful, that's for you lot to figure out.

t_ch: ——回见，蜗居公主、灰子！
t_en: ...So long, Princess Homebody! So long, Gray Mystery!

t_ch: 咦…她就这么消失啦？
t_en: Huh? She disappeared just like that?

t_ch: 赛飞儿阁下一向…随心所欲。但无论如何，她给我们带来了有价值的信息。
t_en: Lady Cipher has always been a... free spirit. In any case, she has brought us some valuable information.

t_ch: 如果万敌阁下此时也在浅滩上，那他一定能成为我们的道标。因为，他和那些离群的亡魂不同…即便在死者的世界，他也一定能找到方向。
t_en: If Lord Mydei is also somewhere along the river shoals right now, then he will certainly become our signpost. After all, he's different from those wandering souls... He will find his way even in the realm of the dead.

t_ch: 说到「方向」…
t_en: Speaking of "finding the way"...

t_ch: …这里该怎么过去？
t_en: ...How are we supposed to get over there?

t_ch: 放心，这时就该人家登场了——
t_en: Don't worry. It's time for me to shine...

t_ch: 附近有{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}的「追忆符文」，使用祷言，让河流回到还没泛滥的时候吧！
t_en: {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx{RUBY_E#}'s Rune of Recollection is nearby. Use prayers to return the river to before it got flooded!

t_ch: 啊…怎么这也有小黑怪，它们还能在冥河里游泳？
t_en: Ah! Why are the black tide monsters here too? Can they swim in the River of Souls?

t_ch: 即便是{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}，恐怕也无法接纳黑潮造物……
t_en: Even {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#} wouldn't be able to accept black tide monsters into their realm...

t_ch: 从这些痕迹来看，冒犯「死亡」…想必要付出沉重的代价。
t_en: These traces seem to suggest that anybody who trespasses upon "Death"... will pay a heavy price.

t_ch: 我们也会落得一样的下场吗？
t_en: Will we suffer the same fate as well?

t_ch: 我们也是来冒犯它的。
t_en: We're also here to trespass upon the Titan.

t_ch: 死者的世界需要安宁，必须剔除这些不和谐的噪音。
t_en: Since the realm of the dead must remain peaceful, it's imperative that we rid the place of such discordant noise.

t_ch: 请退后…我来应对这些敌人。
t_en: Please stay back... Let me deal with these enemies.

t_ch: 这些已经被打倒的家伙，难道是……
t_en: Could these dead monsters actually be...

t_ch: 如果赛飞儿阁下所言属实…或许真是万敌阁下所为。
t_en: If Lady Cipher is to be believed... this could be the handiwork of Lord Mydei.

t_ch: 即便身在冥界，他也在对抗黑潮啊……
t_en: He's still fighting against the black tide, even in the nether realm...

t_ch: 这里也有游荡的亡魂……
t_en: There are wandering souls of the deceased here as well...

t_ch: 我们得先回到「现在」，才能和他们交流。
t_en: We must first return to "the present" before we can communicate with them.

t_ch: 啊，你们的模样…莫非是女王提到的异邦勇士？
t_en: Ah, by the looks of it... you must be the foreign warriors that the queen had mentioned, right?

t_ch: 实在抱歉，此次出征容不得半分疏忽，未能抽身迎接，还请见谅。
t_en: Since the current campaign requires the utmost care, I wasn't able to make time to welcome all of you. Please, forgive me.

t_ch: …异邦的勇士？
t_en: ...Foreign warriors?

t_ch: 一位祭司，一位战士，一位…咳，一只奇兽…和女王的形容完全一样，天赐的救星！
t_en: A priestess, a warrior, and a, um... a divine beast... You look identical to the blessed saviors that the queen described!

t_ch: 我带宠物的，是猎人。
t_en: This is my pet, so I'm actually of the Hunter class.

t_ch: 你在说什么啦！
t_en: What...?

t_ch: 糟了，没人治疗。
t_en: Oh darn. No healer on this team.

t_ch: 「出征」？请问，你们要去征伐谁？
t_en: A campaign? Who are you campaigning against?

t_ch: 现在…还没人知道。
t_en: Nobody knows... as of now.

t_ch: 前去探路的斥候仅有一位得以复返。他说那是不可接近的禁地，是冥府向人界敞开的门扉……
t_en: Of the scouts that went ahead, only one has managed to return. He says that the land ahead is a forbidden land that should not be approached, as it's the doorway between the netherworld and the mortal realm...

t_ch: 而其余的…灵识尽失，日渐腐朽。
t_en: As for the rest of the scouts... They've completely lost their souls and are decaying by the day.

t_ch: 城邦中仍有争议，学者们认为这一灾厄前所未见，应当被冠上新的称呼。
t_en: While there is still dissension within the city, the scholars agree that this calamity was so unprecedented that it needed to be given a new designation.

t_ch: 前所未见的灾厄？莫非是…「死亡」？
t_en: An unprecedented calamity? Could it be... "Death"?

t_ch: 几位果然听说了，当然…「死亡」只是其中一种可能性，暂时还没有定论。
t_en: It seems that you have indeed heard about this. Well... "Death" is just one of the possibilities. It's too early to come to a definitive conclusion.

t_ch: 从未见过「死亡」的世界…看来，这些人们活在灾厄泰坦降临以前的黄金世。
t_en: A world that has never seen "Death"... Looks like these people lived in the Era Chrysea, before the Calamity Titans descended upon the land.

t_ch: 请问…回来的那位斥候，他在哪里？
t_en: May I ask... where is the scout who managed to came back?

t_ch: 他回到了斯缇科西亚…但也只支撑了短短半日。而正是他的见闻，使我们决意集结。
t_en: Though he returned to Styxia... he only had enough left in him to last no more than half a day. However, it was his intel that convinced us to rally together.

t_ch: 在那凶险之地，他说自己窥到了恶龙的魔影。它张开巨口吞噬眼前的一切，将那名为「死亡」之物，带给斯缇科西亚。
t_en: He said that he witnessed the demonic specter of an evil dragon at that treacherous land. The apparition opened its giant maw and devoured everything in sight, and thus brought Styxia that thing called "Death."

t_ch: 斯缇科西亚…带来死亡的恶龙……
t_en: Styxia... an evil dragon that brings Death...

t_ch: 好耳熟？这不是寓言童话的情节吗？
t_en: It all sounds rather familiar... Wait, wasn't this part of that fable?

t_ch: 先生，请问女王和公主…现状如何？
t_en: Sir, could I... ask how the queen and princess are doing?

t_ch: 女王？
t_en: The queen?

t_ch: ——啊，你们的模样…莫非是女王提到的异邦勇士？
t_en: — Ah, by the looks of it... you must be the foreign warriors that the queen had mentioned, right?

t_ch: 复读机？
t_en: Echo?

t_ch: 他刚才好像问过一遍了。
t_en: I think he asked that already.

t_ch: 重复播放了？
t_en: Is he set to loop on repeat?

t_ch: 一样的台词…看来他也被困在生前的片刻了呀。
t_en: It's the same lines... Looks like he's still trapped in the moments before his death.

t_ch: …让我来吧。
t_en: ...Leave it to me.

t_ch: 阁下，战争早已结束了。斯缇科西亚…已经不存在了。
t_en: Sir, the war ended long ago. Styxia... no longer exists.

t_ch: 斯缇科西亚…覆灭了？
t_en: Styxia... is no more?

t_ch: 这、这可不行！新来的勇士，速速去列队吧！
t_en: Th—This cannot stand! Newcomers, hurry up and go take your positions!

t_ch: 这位老人家…看来很难接受现实呀。
t_en: Looks like it's difficult for this gentleman to accept reality.

t_ch: 阁下，我是女王大人派来的「督战圣女」。若你接受我的赐福，今日战事必能顺遂。
t_en: Sir, I am the Maiden of War sent here by the queen herself. If you accept my blessing, then today's battle will be smooth sailing.

t_ch: 圣女？从未听过…时间紧迫，请别耽误太久。
t_en: Maiden of War? I've never heard of this... There's little time to waste. Please don't take too long.

t_ch: 请安心吧，我一向…不愿让这一刻太久。
t_en: Don't worry. I'm typically... unwilling to prolong such a moment.

t_ch: 一切都…结束了？我们…胜利了吗？
t_en: Is it over...? Have we... won?

t_ch: 守城士兵们的亡魂，随着百夫长的「死亡」一同消散了。
t_en: The souls of the city's guards dissipate along with the centurion's "death."

t_ch: 小蝶…你还好吗？
t_en: Cas... Are you all right?

t_ch: 我…没事。只是没有想到，我在死者的世界仍要执行「入殓」……
t_en: I—I'm fine. I just didn't expect that I would still have to "embalm" others even in the realm of the dead...

t_ch: 这是…灵魂的残留吗？
t_en: Is this... a remnant of his soul?

t_ch: 那么，我该如何对您托以信任呢？
t_en: Well, then. Why should I place my trust in you...

t_ch: 远道而来的圣女？
t_en: Holy maiden from afar?

t_ch: 等等，这一幕是……
t_en: W—Wait, this is...

t_ch: 我不明白您的言外之意……
t_en: I don't quite understand what you're trying to say...

t_ch: 神谕已然昭示你的姓名，「死亡」的黄金裔，奥赫玛理应将你奉为贵宾。
t_en: Since the prophecy has already pronounced your name as Chrysos Heir of "Death," Okhema ought to treat you as an esteemed guest.

t_ch: 但你似乎不打算隐瞒自己和元老院有所接触…而我与他们的矛盾，恐怕并非秘密。
t_en: However, you seem to have no intentions of concealing your involvement with the Council of Elders... While most likely being aware of my issues against them.

t_ch: 我无法否认。是那些人为我提供了这间小屋，他们的要求只有一个：希望「死亡」的诅咒能完全为您所用……
t_en: I cannot deny your claim. It was the Council that offered this room to me, and they had only one request... They want the curse of "Death" to be completely subjugated to your will.

t_ch: 我…可以接受。
t_en: I... was agreeable to that request.

t_ch: 愿闻其详。
t_en: But why?

t_ch: 与生俱来的诅咒…我无力让渡。但从今往后，我会听凭您的心意将其运用，绝不会有多余的举动。
t_en: I am... unable to rid myself of this innate curse. However, from this day onward, I shall use it according to your will. I will never make any superfluous moves on my own.

t_ch: 当然，因为这是他们企图掌控逐火之旅的方式——
t_en: I see. So that is how they plan to control the Flame-Chase Journey...

t_ch: 将赐人以死的重责转嫁到我身上。一旦我陷入对处刑的痴醉，他们便有了理由断绝黄金裔的征途。
t_en: They have shifted the heavy responsibility of granting death over to me. If I ever become obsessed with executing people, they will then have ample reason to terminate the Chrysos Heirs' journey.

t_ch: 但您似乎…不以为意？这让我有些意外。
t_en: And yet, you seem to... not mind? This is quite surprising to me.

t_ch: 因为…我等生来背负的宿命，远比个人的生命更为沉重。
t_en: That's because... the fate that we were born with is a burden far heavier than that of an individual's life.

t_ch: 而那不存在于预言之中，却由我纺入命运的，也并非仅此一例。
t_en: I've woven many unforeseen events into the tapestry of fate. This is but one instance.

t_ch: 整理好心爱的衣物，随我启程吧。奥赫玛欢迎你，遐蝶。
t_en: Tidy up your precious garments and come with me. Okhema welcomes you, Castorice.

t_ch: 你们似乎听到了一声满足的叹息，仿佛出自某种庞然巨物之口。
t_en: You seem to hear a sigh of contentment, as if it comes from the mouth of some colossal being.

t_ch: 随后，那往昔的景象倏然散去……
t_en: Then, the scenes of the past abruptly dissipated...

t_ch: 没想到在这里也能找到记忆的残像…大概和{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}的符文有关吧？
t_en: I didn't think that we'd find vestiges of memory here... Maybe they've got something to do with {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx{RUBY_E#}'s rune?

t_ch: …或许吧。但无需它再做提醒，这段记忆我永生难忘。
t_en: ...Perhaps, but I do not need a reminder from them. This memory is one that I will never forget.

t_ch: 元老院已经利用过你一回了。
t_en: So it seems that the Council of Elders has manipulated you once already.

t_ch: 没错…我们之间的关系始终若即若离。但阿格莱雅大人没有因此对我设防…反而对我百般信任。
t_en: Indeed. While my relationship with the Council has always been quite ambiguous, Lady Aglaea has never taken any precautions against me. Instead... She's shown me nothing but complete trust.

t_ch: 阿格莱雅很信任你。
t_en: Aglaea trusts you unreservedly.

t_ch: 我…也想回应她的这份信任。
t_en: I... really want to repay her for that trust.

t_ch: 不过，到底为何，这段记忆会在此时显现…当真是{RUBY_B#岁月之泰坦}欧洛尼斯{RUBY_E#}的影响吗？
t_en: But... why would this memory appear right now? Is this really {RUBY_B#Time Titan}Oronyx{RUBY_E#}'s power?

t_ch: 恕我冒昧，我只是个遥远彼邦来的倒霉学者，跌入冥河，作了亡魂……
t_en: Please excuse me. I am but a scholar from a distant land who had the misfortune of falling into the River of Souls and turned into a member of the dead...

t_ch: 欸？这个人…好像知道自己已经死了。
t_en: Huh? This one... seems to know that she's already dead.

t_ch: 只是推测，但既然您这样说，看来我猜对了。
t_en: Oh, I was merely speculating. But it seems I was right.

t_ch: 还有，这边这位…我能认出来，你是哀地里亚的「死亡」圣女吧？
t_en: And you... You... I recognize you. You're the Holy Maiden of "Death" from Aidonia, right?

t_ch: 对不起…我无法令你死而复生。但你若有所求，我可以结束你漫长的困顿。
t_en: I'm sorry... I cannot bring you back to life. However, should you desire it, I can end your prolonged limbo.

t_ch: 哈哈，我知道。即便你能让我活过来，我也不会这么选。
t_en: Hah, I'm well aware of that. Even if you were able to revive me, I would never choose that option.

t_ch: 因为我并非不慎跌入冥河，而是为了求死一跃而下。
t_en: That's because I didn't fall into the River of Souls by accident. Instead, I leaped into it to seek my death.

t_ch: …这是为何？
t_en: ...Why?

t_ch: 您若能窥得我的一生，想必不会苛责。那实在是…令人苦不堪言。
t_en: If you could see the life I've led, then you would not reproach me so. My life... was far too miserable.

t_ch: 但我没想到，死后竟也一样。多么可笑，我逃避尘世，转而踏入了持续千年的苦痛。
t_en: But I did not expect that things stay the same even in death! What a joke! In escaping my mortal life, I've entered a millennium of pain instead.

t_ch: 瑟希斯在上，人究竟是有多么怯懦，竟同时畏惧着生和死？
t_en: Cerces above! What cowards humans are, for we fear both life and death simultaneously.

t_ch: 抱歉…这个问题我同样无法回答。
t_en: I'm sorry... I'm also powerless to answer that question.

t_ch: 无妨，圣女，至少您回应了我的期许…请将「死亡」赐给我吧。
t_en: That's alright, Holy Maiden. At least you are able to respond to me. Please... grant me "Death."

t_ch: 瑟希斯在上，这是否算作…思考到了最后一刻呢？
t_en: Cerces above... Does this count as thinking till the moment I die?

t_ch: 她似乎…解脱了。
t_en: She seems... free.

t_ch: 无法想象在此游荡百年的空虚…
t_en: I can't imagine what it'd feel like to roam around this void for centuries...

t_ch: 我见过太多的离别…她的苦恼并不罕见。也有人会为看似微小的烦忧终结自己的生命。
t_en: I have seen too many farewells... Her distress is quite commonplace. There are those who will end their lives over what seems to be the smallest of worries.

t_ch: 在有些人看来…被我视作诅咒的力量，反而是一种「赐福」。
t_en: For some people... This power that I possess is a "blessing" rather than a curse.

t_ch: 但…我从未说服自己接受这种想法。
t_en: ...But I never managed to convince myself of that.

t_ch: 小蝶…还是想创造一个没有「死亡」的世界吗？
t_en: Cas... Are you still trying to create a world without "Death"?

t_ch: 嗯。因为在无限的时光尺度下，没有什么烦忧是无法被解决的……
t_en: Yes. Because... When placed under time's vast scales, there is no worry in this world that cannot be solved.

t_ch: 你该和丹恒聊聊这件事。
t_en: You should chat with Dan Heng about this.

t_ch: 我无法苟同。
t_en: I can't agree with you on this.

t_ch: 这是我秉持千年的心愿，阁下。或许曾有人接近令我改变想法……
t_en: This is a wish that I've held for almost a millennium. My mind might have changed once upon a time if people managed to strike up a friendship with me...

t_ch: …但自从听说你已濒临死去的消息，我就更加确信——为这世间抹除「死亡」便是我的使命。
t_en: But I've never been more firm in my conviction, now that I've learned about your near-death state — It is my duty to wipe "Death" from the face of this world.

t_ch: 好大——好空旷呀！这是王国的宝库吗？
t_en: Wow... This place is so spacious! Is this the kingdom's vault?

t_ch: 该使用欧洛尼斯的奇迹了！
t_en: It's time to use Oronyx's miracle!

t_ch: 用归用…人家可要约法三章，我们回到过去不是为了偷东西。
t_en: That's fine and all, but we need to lay down some rules. We're not gonna return to the past to steal things.

t_ch: 看见这些立体结构就害怕。
t_en: I get scared when I see these vertical structures.

t_ch: 楼梯断掉了，道路也断掉了，这算是危房吧……
t_en: Broken stairs, blocked paths... This place is gonna fall down soon...

t_ch: 通往更高处的门，是那里吗？好像有人…有魂守着呢。
t_en: Do we need to enter that doorway to climb even higher? There seems to be a person... more like a soul guarding it.

t_ch: 别、别过来……
t_en: D—Don't come near me...

t_ch: 咦，他怎么那么惊慌？是被黑潮造物吓到了吗？
t_en: Huh? Why does he look so panicked? Did the black tide monsters frighten him?

t_ch: 还是说…他是被自己惊人的威力给吓坏了？
t_en: Or... is he actually scared of his own extraordinary power?

t_ch: 我来和他比划比划！
t_en: Lemme fight him!

t_ch: 这就是我们强者的烦恼…
t_en: Ah, these are the troubles that powerhouses like us face...

t_ch: 不必惊慌，我们只是过路人。您看，没有哪位会显出凶神恶煞之相。
t_en: There's no need to be alarmed. We're just passing by the area. See? None of us look threatening.

t_ch: 这倒的确…不过……
t_en: You have a point, although...

t_ch: 虽说这里曾是王室宝库，但如今早已被劫掠一空…请各位及早离去吧！
t_en: While this might once have been the royal vault, it's since been plundered empty... So, please go away right this instant!

t_ch: 您误会了…我们只是路过时听到了您的喊声，因此前来看看。
t_en: You've misunderstood us... We simply heard your shouts as we were passing by, then decided to come over to find out what happened.

t_ch: 天，我叫出来了？实在是失态……
t_en: My goodness, did I really yell out loud? How rude of me...

t_ch: 劳各位费心，我只是见到了一位…勇士。那亡魂没有伤害我，但他的重拳…实在惊人。
t_en: I'm sorry to have bothered you. I merely saw a... warrior. While that spirit didn't harm me, his blows... really packed a hefty punch.

t_ch: 勇士？难道……
t_en: A warrior? Could it be...

t_ch: 等等，几位似乎和他不同…你们是生者？你们怎可能活着在此处行走？
t_en: Wait a minute. You seem to be quite different from him... Are you actually alive? How can you walk around here while alive?

t_ch: 说来话长…我们是来此觐见{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}的。
t_en: It's a long story... We're here to seek an audience with {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: {RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}？那是何物？
t_en: {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#}? Who's that?

t_ch: 莫非…他也活在「死亡」降临前的时代？
t_en: Is it possible that... he's also living in the era before "Death" descended?

t_ch: 「死亡」…难道你们是来讨伐恶龙玻吕刻斯的？
t_en: "Death"... Could it be that you are here to defeat the evil dragon Pollux?

t_ch: 玻吕刻斯…它就是塞纳…不，「死亡」的化身？
t_en: Pollux... Is that the embodiment of Thana — no, "Death"?

t_ch: 据百夫长带领的勇士们说，那恶龙早已死去，致人于死地的恶咒并非由它而起……
t_en: According to the warriors under the centurion's command, that evil dragon has been dead for quite a while. The curse that condemns people to death didn't originate from it...

t_ch: 而在龙的怀中，他们见到了一位少女…她才是真正的死神！
t_en: But they saw a girl within the dragon's embrace... And she is the true Reaper!

t_ch: …请问，那位女孩身在何处？
t_en: ...Pray tell, where is that girl right now?

t_ch: 据说，女王不计代价将她带回城堡，她意欲以这前所未见的伟力，将整个翁法罗斯纳入掌中……
t_en: I've heard that the queen spared no effort in bringing her back to the castle. Her Majesty intends to control all of Amphoreus with this unprecedented power...

t_ch: 至于真假…就没人知道了。
t_en: As for whether this rumor is true... Well, no one knows.

t_ch: 您的见闻十分宝贵，感激不尽。
t_en: You have my thanks for the invaluable insight that you've given us.

t_ch: 请将此触…视作对您的谢礼吧。
t_en: Please treat this touch... as a token of my gratitude.

t_ch: 你…难道就是…
t_en: You... Are you...

t_ch: 谢谢……
t_en: Thank you...

t_ch: 这是，刚才的……
t_en: This is his...

t_ch: 所以，你便将死亡带给了他？
t_en: Then, you granted him death?

t_ch: 这不像你…即便是我向你下令，你应当也会拒绝才是。
t_en: That isn't like you... You would have refused even if I commanded you to do so.

t_ch: 我…不清楚。
t_en: I... don't know.

t_ch: 我本是出于同情，以为自己不会因此痛苦不堪。可如今仍然…心乱如麻。
t_en: I did it out of sympathy, believing that I wouldn't be racked with agony, but... I am still in a state of turmoil.

t_ch: 同情？同情那个战无不胜的少年人？
t_en: Sympathy? Sympathy for that youth who triumphed in every battle?

t_ch: 那位少年，他受到了{RUBY_B#律法之泰坦}塔兰顿{RUBY_E#}的眷顾，从神手中习得了一式「秘剑」。借此，他在与人的争斗中可以一击制敌，永不落败。
t_en: That young man was favored by {RUBY_B#Law Titan}Talanton{RUBY_E#}, who taught him a move that will defeat any mortal enemy with a single blow and emerge victorious every time.

t_ch: 在黄金世，他可凭此技常胜。但如今，死亡降临世间，那神赐的秘剑，也使他能随意「致人于死」，换言之……
t_en: He could rely on that move to always achieve victory during Era Chrysea. However, now that death has descended upon the mortal realm, that divine swordplay also means he can send others to death at will. In other words...

t_ch: 你觉得他…有些像你？
t_en: You feel that he's similar... to you?

t_ch: 嗯，他永不会技差一筹，但凡出手，对方必然死去。
t_en: Yes. Since he can never be inferior to others in martial combat, death would be the inevitable outcome every time he strikes.

t_ch: 所以他找到了我，请求我…了结这一切。
t_en: That's why he approached me and pleaded... Pleaded for me to put an end to all of this.

t_ch: 或许是与「死亡」的和解来得太过突然，你才会感到无助。
t_en: You are perhaps feeling lost from making your peace with "Death" in so abrupt a manner.

t_ch: 我…并未找到和解。
t_en: I... have not made peace with death.

t_ch: 我亲见哀地里亚的人们，在死亡面前挺起了胸膛。在命中注定的终局来临前…他们满足于生命已然谱写的一切。
t_en: I've seen the people of Aidonia stand tall and proud in the face of death. Even before the fated finale arrived, they were already content with the lives that they'd lived...

t_ch: 他们的神情如此骄傲，以至于让我认为…假若「死亡」真的只是某位神明无心降下的权职，那对他们的尊严是种侮辱。
t_en: They had such proud expressions... Expressions that made me believe "Death" would be an insult to these mortals if the concept is truly a power that a god had carelessly cast down upon the world.

t_ch: 或许在这世间，死亡的权柄…本就不该存在。一如我…也不该存在。
t_en: Perhaps death's authority was never meant to exist in this world... just like how I shouldn't exist.

t_ch: 我不想贸然否认你的理论。不过…不妨听我为你讲述一个故事。
t_en: I won't refute your beliefs for now, but would you be interested in listening to a story of mine?

t_ch: 那是一个来自异邦的童话。在那冰原中，那个将你带给我的人，曾为我讲述了这一切——
t_en: It's a fairy tale from foreign lands, told by the person who brought you to me in that ice field long ago...

t_ch: 那位…斯缇科西亚的女王。
t_en: ...A tale told by the Queen of Styxia.

t_ch: 比它的出现更加突然，旧事戛然而止……
t_en: More sudden than its appearance, the past comes to an abrupt halt...

t_ch: 你怀疑自己有些幻听——那满足的叹息似乎又响起过一次。
t_en: You begin to suspect that you might be hearing things — that satisfying sigh seems to have echoed once more.

t_ch: 啊？等等等等……
t_en: What? W—W—W—Wait a minute...

t_ch: 所以，小蝶…从巨龙怀中诞生的少女，那些幽灵口中「死亡」的化身…就是你？！
t_en: That means... The girl born in the dragon's embrace... The personification of "Death" these souls talked about... That's you? Cas???

t_ch: 从刚才开始…我就隐约有所预感。但斯缇科西亚的一切，我的确全然不知……
t_en: I've already had an inkling for a while now... But I honestly have no memories of Styxia...

t_ch: 我的记忆…应该是从阿蒙内特将我带到哀地里亚时开始才对。
t_en: My memories only begin... After Amunet brought me to Aidonia.

t_ch: 别回头，向前、向前。
t_en: Don't look back... Keep moving forward, always.

t_ch: 又是亡语蝶…我们离冥界越来越近了吗？
t_en: It's yet another Eulogistic Butterfly... Are we getting closer to the nether realm?

t_ch: 喔…莫非是斯缇科西亚的后人？
t_en: Oh... Could you be the scions of Styxia?

t_ch: 你们听闻了我在此进行的伟业，特来送上炼成的最后一样材料？
t_en: Did you hear about the great work that I'm doing here, and have thus arrived to give me the last material that I need for transmutation?

t_ch: 伟业？
t_en: Great work?

t_ch: 玻吕刻斯的大名，各位可曾听过？
t_en: Have you heard of the great name of Pollux, perchance?

t_ch: 它的驾驶员可能就在这里。
t_en: Its pilot is probably right here.

t_ch: 不必那样形容，{NICKNAME}阁下……
t_en: You don't have to describe it like that, {M#Mr.}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME}...

t_ch: 今天倒是听过很多。
t_en: I've definitely heard that name many times today.

t_ch: 听过？那就好说了。
t_en: You have? Then that's good.

t_ch: 在那神话的年代里，或许你们还尚未出世，但在炼金术的领域，已无人能够望我项背。我将在这冥河流经的浅滩，使玻吕刻斯再度腾飞…让「死亡」复归于世！
t_en: During the age of myth, a time likely before your birth, I was the unsurpassed zenith of alchemy that none could reach! On the shoals of the River of Souls, I shall make Pollux soar again... and bring "Death" back to the world!

t_ch: 但…「死亡」不就是由它带来世间的吗？
t_en: But... didn't that dragon already bring "Death" to the world?

t_ch: 喔…看来你们有所不知。
t_en: Oh...? Looks like you don't know the whole picture.

t_ch: 的确，那不知所踪的少女能致人于死。而将她送来世间的玻吕刻斯…偏偏又会让人「无法死去」啊。
t_en: While it's true that the missing girl can grant death to mortals... Pollux, the one who delivered her onto this world... Makes it so that humankind "cannot die."

t_ch: 所以…果然如此吗？
t_en: So... It's just as I feared...

t_ch: 解释一下你的猜想？
t_en: What did you figure out?

t_ch: 你和龙…是生死的两面？
t_en: You and that dragon... Are you the two sides of the same coin?

t_ch: 这是我的猜测……
t_en: This is my guess...

t_ch: 死难者本应顺着冥河漂流，直至到达西风的尽头。但这条灵魂的河流…却因玻吕刻斯的陨落阻断了。
t_en: The deceased are supposed to drift down the River of Souls until they reach the end of the west winds, but this river of souls was blocked... by Pollux's fall.

t_ch: 不错！所以，我才必须让它重回世间，展翅高飞。我早已在高塔布下炼金法阵，只可惜…缺失了最关键的一样材料。
t_en: That's right! That's why it's imperative that I bring the dragon back to the mortal realm and make it soar the skies again! I have long since prepared my alchemical matrix in the tower, although... I am still missing the most crucial material of all.

t_ch: 那最后一样祭品——便是与死亡共生的少女！
t_en: That final ingredient... The final sacrifice... Is none other than the girl who lives out her days with death!

t_ch: 咦，怎么回事？小蝶你应该还没伸手吧……
t_en: Huh? What's going on? You didn't touch him, did you...?

t_ch: 诸位，我晓得自己已无力回天。拿去这个，求你们代我实现那至高的伟业……
t_en: I know that I am no longer capable of carrying out that task. Take this, all of you, and please, fulfill that great undertaking on my behalf...

t_ch: 这是……
t_en: This...

t_ch: 我们在{RUBY_B#海洋之泰坦}法吉娜{RUBY_E#}的盛宴中酣醉了太久，才开始寻求神赐的「死亡」，好让自己摆脱命运的无常…然而，我们反受其害。
t_en: We had been inebriated in {RUBY_B#Ocean Titan}Phagousa{RUBY_E#}'s feast for too long before we began to seek divine-bestowed "Death," so that we could be freed from the whims of fate... However, that brought doom upon us.

t_ch: 我们没能唤来尊神，却迎来了横绝冥河的魔物。那可憎的巨龙，玻吕刻斯，将所有亡者拒于冥界之外。
t_en: Instead of summoning a supreme god, we encountered a monster that cut off the River of Souls. That detestable dragon Pollux denied all the dead from the gates of the nether realm.

t_ch: 求死不得的灵魂如潮水般席卷了斯缇科西亚。我的城邦、我的人民，如今已不复存在……
t_en: Unable to rest in peace, these departed spirits engulfed Styxia like a roaring tide. As a result, my city and its people no longer exist...

t_ch: 那女婴是我唯一能救下的幸存者。她诞生在恶龙腹中，生来便怀有「死亡」的天赋。她一定是诸神赐给我们赎罪的机会……
t_en: That child is the only person whom I managed to save. Born within the evil dragon's abdomen, she has possessed the talent of "Death" since her birth. She must have been granted to us by the gods as a chance for us to atone for our sins...

t_ch: 请你一定要将她抚养成人…别让她再经历我们所遭遇的苦难。
t_en: Please, you must nurture her to adulthood... and prevent her from experiencing all of the suffering that we went through.

t_ch: …我答应你。我将如母亲一般照料她成长，守护她不为「死亡」所害。
t_en: ...I promise that I will care for her as a mother, that I will protect her from the evils of "Death."

t_ch: {RUBY_B#海洋之泰坦}法吉娜{RUBY_E#}在上，感谢你的善举……
t_en: By {RUBY_B#Ocean Titan}Phagousa{RUBY_E#} above, thank you for your kindness...

t_ch: 身为母亲，请你为这女孩赐下姓名…在我被冥河卷走以前，至少让我知道，在来世应当如何呼唤她吧。
t_en: As her mother, please bless this child with a name... Before I am swept away by the River of Souls, please let me know how I should address her in my next life.

t_ch: 就像蝴蝶，落在「死亡」的枝头……
t_en: Like how a transient raven butterfly, a fleeting papilio castor, perches on the twig of "Death"...

t_ch: 「遐蝶」…我想，这就是她的名字。
t_en: "Castorice"... That shall be her name.

t_ch: 一如既往，灵魂的幻象于斯消散。
t_en: As always, the illusion of spirits dissipates here.

t_ch: 如同潮水褪去，一声轻叹随潮声消散在月夜中。
t_en: Like the retreating tide, a soft sigh fades away with the sound of the waves in the moonlit night.

t_ch: 这是…我的名字…？
t_en: That's... where my name comes from?

t_ch: *远方传来的巨大轰鸣*
t_en: *A massive roar in the distance*

t_ch: 又又又来了…这究竟是什么动静？
t_en: Th—Th—The sound's back... What is making that ruckus?

t_ch: 生者，别浪费时间较量口舌了。冥界的「怒火」将至……
t_en: Living ones, don't waste your time bickering. The nether realm's "wrath" is falling upon you...

t_ch: 黑潮造物…从什么时候……
t_en: Black tide creations... When did they...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}阁下，请小心迎战——
t_en: {M#Mr.}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME}, please fight them with caution...

t_ch: …小心落得和那些黑潮造物同样的下场！
t_en: ...Be careful not to suffer the same fate as those black tide creatures!

t_ch: …各位，快躲到我身后！
t_en: ...Everyone, hide behind me!

t_ch: 好多…大家还撑得住吗？
t_en: There's so many of them... How are we holding up?

t_ch: 不过…「纷争」……
t_en: Mere... Morsels...

t_ch: 凯旋人间…余兴而已！
t_en: For the feast of "Strife"... Upon their triumphant return!

t_ch: 巨大的轰响过后，一个雄壮的身影立于你们面前——
t_en: After the rumble, a towering figure stands before you...

t_ch: 那大概便是炼金术士口中的冥界之怒。他该有多么可怕，一定比你见到过的任何事物都更面目可憎，都更……
t_en: That is probably the wrath of the nether realm, as spoken of by the alchemists. How terrifying must it be? It must be more hideous than anything you've ever encountered, more...

t_ch: 都更…面熟。
t_en: More... familiar.

t_ch: …你们……
t_en: You...?

t_ch: 万敌…阁下？
t_en: Lord Mydei...?

t_ch: 我可想死你啦…呃，双关了。
t_en: I missed you so much that it almost killed me... Uh. Pun unintended.

t_ch: 你也来冥河探险啊？
t_en: You're adventuring in the River of Souls, too?

t_ch: 万敌阁下…你又经历了一番死战啊。
t_en: Lord Mydei... You've been through some tough battles once again.

t_ch: 那黑潮…无穷无尽。我的死亡…只是临时。
t_en: That black tide... is endless. My death... is only temporary.

t_ch: 我会找到归时的路…回到生者的世界…继续我的「守护」。
t_en: I will find my path of return... and go back to the world of the living... and continue my "protection."

t_ch: 万敌阁下，我一直都想知道…死亡为何会抗拒你？
t_en: Lord Mydei, I have always wanted to... Why does Death reject you?

t_ch: 是我…在抗拒它。顺流而下的诱惑…我会抵抗……
t_en: I'm the one... resisting it. I must resist... the temptation to go with the flow...

t_ch: 逆流而行，杀出这该死的冥界……
t_en: I'll go against the current... and kill my way out of this cursed nether realm...

t_ch: 那是…我的宿命。
t_en: That is... my destiny.

t_ch: 他说话有些磕磕巴巴的…
t_en: He seems to be stuttering...

t_ch: 这真是那个霸道的万敌吗？
t_en: Is this really the proud Mydei?

t_ch: 他似乎在用全部的精神和亡语的诱惑搏斗…为了拒绝在这一刻迎接死亡。
t_en: It seems like he's completely focused on resisting the temptation of the deads' whispers... He's working hard every minute to deny death.

t_ch: 原来…他的战场不止在生者的世界。
t_en: It seems... his battlefield is not limited to the world of the living.

t_ch: 万敌阁下，你愿意与我们同行吗？
t_en: Lord Mydei, are you willing to travel with us?

t_ch: 我们将前往那高塔上的法阵…去唤出那藏匿已久的{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}。
t_en: We're headed for the matrix inside that tower... to summon {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#} from their prolonged concealment.

t_ch: {RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳…托斯……{RUBY_E#}
t_en: {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Tha... natos...{RUBY_E#}

t_ch: 挑战…死亡。
t_en: Challenge... death.

t_ch: 没错。如果我们能齐心战胜它…那你也再不必为不死的诅咒所扰了。
t_en: That's right. If we can work together and defeat them... You won't have to be troubled by the curse of immortality any longer.

t_ch: 同行吧，遐蝶……
t_en: Let's go together, Castorice...

t_ch: 就由已与死亡擦肩过千万次之人…为你们引路……
t_en: Let the person who has brushed against death countless times... guide your way...

t_ch: 小蝶…确定要这么做吗？那个炼金术士还说，要把你当成祭品……
t_en: Cas... Are you sure you want to do this? The alchemist said he wanted you to be a sacrifice...

t_ch: …我不害怕。我们已经走到了这一步…绝无再回头的理由。
t_en: ...I'm not afraid. There's no reason for us to turn back anymore.

t_ch: 「比起犹豫，还是启程更好。也许就在今天，命运会恻隐一时，抛下些许仁慈。」
t_en: "Rather than standing still, taking action is by far the better option. Perhaps fate might take pity on us today and show some compassion."

t_ch: 「追忆符文」装置并没有任何反应。
t_en: The Rune of Recollection device does not respond.

t_ch: 唔，附近好像也没有其他能用的了……
t_en: Hmm, there doesn't seem to be anything else nearby that can be used...

t_ch: 在你们面前是早已锈损断掉的锁链，那是原本能够去向高塔的通路。
t_en: Before you are rusted and broken chains, remnants of a path that once led to the tower.

t_ch: …这可难办了。
t_en: ...This is going to be difficult.

t_ch: 看我跳过去！
t_en: Watch me jump over!

t_ch: …阁下请冷静。若是不慎跌进了冰冷的冥河…您恐怕就真的会溺亡于此。
t_en: ...Please calm down. If you were to fall into the icy cold River of Souls... I'm afraid that you really would die here.

t_ch: 悬锋城是不是也有这个…
t_en: Didn't Castrum Kremnos also have this...?

t_ch: 这样的机关…我熟悉。
t_en: I'm familiar... with this kind of mechanism.

t_ch: 但我从未维修过…断裂的锁链。
t_en: But I've never repaired... broken chains.

t_ch: 呜…人家要是再厉害点，就可以四肢各抓一个，带你们一块飞过去了。
t_en: Ugh... If I was more powerful, I would be able to carry one person with each limb and fly the four of us to the other side.

t_ch: 明明这里有着「追忆符文」，却到处找不见「奇迹宝珠」，真奇怪……
t_en: The Rune of Recollection is here, but I can't find a Miracle Orb anywhere. So weird...

t_ch: 莫非，是在远处那座崩塌的塔楼里？
t_en: Could it be in that collapsed tower in the distance?

t_ch: 出风头的时候到了。
t_en: Looks like it's your time to shine.

t_ch: 关键时刻，还是得人家出马呀…稍等，我去一探究竟！
t_en: It's still up to me to step up in a crisis... Wait here. I'll go check things out!

t_ch: 养迷迷千日，用迷迷一时。
t_en: One minute of Mem being useful comes from a thousand days of training Mem.

t_ch: 听着怪怪的…算了，都交给人家吧！
t_en: That sounds so weird... Forget it. Just leave it to me!

t_ch: 需要人家帮助的时候，就大声叫我吧！
t_en: Just shout for me when you need my help!

t_ch: 去吧，迷迷！
t_en: Go, Mem!

t_ch: 拜托了，迷迷！
t_en: Please, Mem!

t_ch: 收到，交给人家吧！
t_en: Got it. Just leave it to me!

t_ch: 哇，人好多呀！
t_en: Wow, there are so many of you!

t_ch: 小小蝶，小小灰！还有…小小敌，和粉色小狗！
t_en: It's Little Cas, and Little Gray! There's also... Little De and the pink puppy!

t_ch: 小缇安？你不是已经……
t_en: Trianne? Aren't you...

t_ch: …已经牺牲了吗？
t_en: ...Dead?

t_ch: 缇安，还有万敌…我明白了。
t_en: Trianne and Mydei... I get it now.

t_ch: 什、什么？人家怎么越来越混乱了……
t_en: G—Get what? I'm getting even more confused...
Heh...

t_ch: 这还是…第一次在冥河逆流而上时，遇见亡故的亲友啊。
t_en: This is... the first time I've encountered a deceased friend while going upstream in the River of Souls.

t_ch: 果然…是这样。
t_en: It really... works like this.

t_ch: 正如方才途中那些亡灵…我也没能从缇安大人身上感知到生者的气息。
t_en: Just like the other departed spirits we've encountered along the way... I'm also unable to sense the aura of the living on Lady Trianne.

t_ch: 嗯！大家，说得对。缇安早就知道，自己回不到大家身边啦。
t_en: Yup! You're right. Trianne already knows that she'll not be able to return to everyone's side.

t_ch: 但是，缇安相信小小蝶，总有一天会回到，冥界——
t_en: But Trianne believed that Little Cas would eventually return to the nether realm...

t_ch: 所以，缇安一直站在这里，离人间最近的地方，等着小小蝶！
t_en: So Trianne has been standing here, at this spot closest to the world of the living, and waiting for Little Cas!

t_ch: 真是…太辛苦了。
t_en: It must have been... very tough for you.

t_ch: 不过…听小缇安的意思，我们确实也快要到冥界的入口了。
t_en: But according to what Trianne said... We're almost at the entrance of the nether realm.

t_ch: 缇安答应过，缇宝，缇宁。等小小蝶来到这里，缇安要准备好礼物，第一个跑来迎接你。
t_en: Trianne promised Tribbie and Trinnon that when Little Cas got here, Trianne would get the gift ready and be the first person to run over and welcome you.

t_ch: 所以，小小蝶，不要哭！请你，收下这个——
t_en: So, Little Cas, don't cry! Please accept this...

t_ch: ——*我们*，终于把它完成啦！
t_en: ...We finally finished it!

t_ch: 这是，缇宝大人说过的……
t_en: This is what Lady Tribbie once mentioned...

t_ch: 您先前没能完成的…为我准备的礼物……
t_en: The gift for me... that you didn't manage to finish...

t_ch: 雅努斯的圣女…即便阴阳两隔，依旧心意相通。
t_en: The Holy Maiden of Janus... Even when separated by life and death, their thoughts are still connected.

t_ch: 缇安，画了好久！希望小小蝶，喜欢。
t_en: Trianne spent a lot of time drawing this! Trianne hopes that Little Cas likes it.

t_ch: 嗯，真是……
t_en: Mmm... Seriously...

t_ch: 缇安大人，画得真好啊……
t_en: It's seriously... A wonderful drawing, Lady Trianne...

t_ch: 这便是…我们的「逐火之旅」……
t_en: This is... our "Flame-Chase Journey"...

t_ch: 小小蝶，虽然我们不知道，你之前的旅途是什么样。缇宝总说，那一定是很重很重，很让人害怕的旅途。
t_en: Little Cas, we don't know what your journey was like before. Tribbie has always said that it must have been a very harsh and frightening journey.

t_ch: 但是，小小蝶可以不用再害怕：因为，在逐火的旅途里，我们大家，都陪在你的身边！
t_en: But Little Cas no longer has anything to fear now, because everyone is here with you in this Flame-Chase Journey!

t_ch: 是啊……
t_en: That's right...

t_ch: 看到缇安大人的礼物，我才发现…自从踏上逐火之旅，一路走来，我已经拥有了太多曾经不敢想象的一切。
t_en: Only now, when I see Lady Trianne's gift, do I realize that I've already obtained so many things I would never have dared to dream of since I embarked on the Flame-Chase Journey.

t_ch: 我第一次有了很多同伴，第一次有了手工和留影的爱好；第一次和他人共进晚餐，第一次走进市场，买下喜欢了很久的奇美拉娃娃，抱着它进入梦乡……
t_en: It's the first time I've gained so many companions, the first time I picked up the hobbies of crafting and photography, the first time I had dinner with someone else, the first time I went to the market to buy the chimera doll I'd liked for a very long time and hugged it to sleep...

t_ch: 第一次穿上阿格莱雅大人亲手编织的礼服，第一次和缇宝、缇安、缇宁大人散步，第一次和风堇小姐吹着晚风看星星，第一次和{NICKNAME}照料大地兽……
t_en: The first time I wore a dress personally made by Lady Aglaea... the first time I went for a walk with Lady Tribbie, Lady Trianne, and Lady Trinnon... the first time I enjoyed the night breeze and looked up at the stars with Miss Hyacine... the first time I took care of dromases with {NICKNAME}...

t_ch: 这许许多多我不曾奢望的第一次，回忆起来的时候…我的生命好像从数百年、数十年，变成了每一分，每一秒……
t_en: Looking back at all these experiences I never thought were possible... it feels as though the scales of my life had shrunk from centuries and decades down to minutes and seconds, and all of them are so precious...

t_ch: 明明拍过很多照片…却都是些黑白色调的风景，都没留下一张和大家的合照。
t_en: I took countless photos in the past, but they're all black-and-white landscapes. I don't have a single one with friends.

t_ch: 早知道…应该多拍一些的啊……
t_en: If I had known... I would have taken more pictures...

t_ch: 那，现在要拍一张吗？
t_en: Then shall we take one now?

t_ch: 缇安，可以！缇安喜欢小小蝶，喜欢和小小蝶一起拍照。
t_en: Trianne says yes! Trianne likes Little Cas and would love to take a picture with Little Cas!

t_ch: 可惜……
t_en: Sadly...

t_ch: 你我，皆为亡魂…无法在人间留影。
t_en: Being departed souls, our images cannot be captured in the world of the living.

t_ch: …我想也是。
t_en: ...I thought so, too.

t_ch: 话都说到这份上了，却还要留下遗憾…这种事情不要啊！
t_en: What? Are we seriously going to leave any room for regret?

t_ch: 不会有遗憾的……
t_en: There won't be any regrets...

t_ch: 这幅五彩缤纷的画…已胜过人世间任何一张留影了。
t_en: This vivid drawing surpasses any photograph from the world of the living.

t_ch: 小小蝶笑了！缇宝、缇宁、缇安的心愿，终于完成了。
t_en: Little Cas smiled! Tribbie, Trinnon, and Trianne's wish has finally come true.

t_ch: 还记得您说过，命运不是生长在道路尽头的花朵，而是人们自己踏出的小径…现在，属于我的那一条行径，即将来到终点的门关。
t_en: I remember you once said that fate is not the flowers that bloom at the end of the road, but where people tread beyond the gate... Now, the path that belongs to me is about to reach the end.

t_ch: 感谢您…为我带来了最后的钥匙。
t_en: Thank you... for bringing me the final key.

t_ch: 钥匙？小小蝶…你说话怎么变得难懂起来啦？
t_en: Key? Little Cas, why are you talking in riddles all of a sudden?

t_ch: 我已有预感：不久后，您这份礼物将成为挽救{NICKNAME}阁下生命的关键。
t_en: I have a feeling that soon, this gift of yours will become the key to saving {M#Mr.}{F#Miss} {NICKNAME}'s life.

t_ch: 喔，那真是太好啦。既然这样，那就带着笑容，勇敢前进吧！
t_en: Wow! That's amazing! Then keep smiling and carry on bravely!

t_ch: 小小蝶，小小灰，还有大家——明天见！
t_en: Little Cas, Little Gray, everyone — see you tomorrow!

t_ch: 留下一道灿烂的笑容，缇安金色的身影在斯缇科西亚的月夜下，消散于薄雾中。
t_en: With a blinding smile, Trianne's golden form disappears in the thin fog of Styxia's moonlit night.

t_ch: 缇安大人……
t_en: Lady Trianne...

t_ch: 此处的气息…也确实便是冥流的终点。
t_en: The aura of this place... We have indeed reached the end of the Stream of Souls.

t_ch: 继续前进…我或许会迷失于此。我必须离去了…因为黑潮还在生者的世界肆虐。
t_en: If I go any further... I might lose myself. I must leave now, for the black tide still wreaks havoc in the realm of the living.

t_ch: 你能找到离开的路吗？
t_en: Can you find your way out?

t_ch: 千万次…我已挣脱过死亡的枷锁。此次…也不例外。
t_en: I have broken free from the chains of death countless times... This time will be no different.

t_ch: 不一起见证塞纳托斯的真相吗？
t_en: Don't you want to know the truth behind Thanatos?

t_ch: 步入冥界之门…非我所求。
t_en: I seek not to enter the door of the nether realm.

t_ch: 遐蝶…你会为逐火…完成使命么？
t_en: Castorice... Will you fulfill your duty for the Flame-Chase?

t_ch: 我还在思量，万敌阁下。
t_en: I have yet to make my decision, Lord Mydei.
Heh...

t_ch: 无妨…无愧本心地选择吧。就算要再等上千年……
t_en: No matter... Make the choice your heart desires. Even if it takes a thousand years of waiting...

t_ch: 我会一直战斗至死…然后死而复生…直到奇迹降临之刻。
t_en: I will continue to fight till I die... Then rise from death... til the miracle descends.

t_ch: 万敌阁下…还有一件事，或许你应该知晓。
t_en: There is one more thing you should know, Lord Mydei.

t_ch: …何事？
t_en: ...What is it?

t_ch: 那刻夏老师或许推导出了「再创世」的原理。你我…也许将在下一世化为「纷争」与「死亡」，成为降在世间的灾厄。
t_en: Professor Anaxa may have deduced the principles behind the Era Nova. You and I... may be reincarnated as "Strife" and "Death" in the next cycle, becoming calamities wreaking havoc upon the world.

t_ch: …我知晓了。
t_en: ...I see.

t_ch: …只有这样吗？你愿意坦然接受…这样的命运吗？
t_en: ...Is that all? Are you going to accept such a fate without complaints?

t_ch: 你…有任何策略，能让我们击碎命运的管束？
t_en: Do you have... a strategy to shatter the shackles of our fate?

t_ch: 我…还没找到方法。也许不可能找得到……
t_en: I... haven't found it yet. Perhaps I never will...

t_ch: 那就…别再思考无用之事。
t_en: Then... cease wasting your thoughts on futile matters.

t_ch: 掌控…你能掌控之事。待那无可逃避的命运降临时……
t_en: Control what little you can. And when fate's inescapable grasp comes...

t_ch: 再用你强有力的意志…反抗它的摆弄。
t_en: Resist it with the strength of your mighty will.

t_ch: 感谢你，万敌阁下。那个正确的选择…我会找到它的。
t_en: Thank you, Lord Mydei. I will find the right decision.

t_ch: 以「纷争」之名…愿你死得其所。
t_en: In the name of "Strife"... may you meet a fitting end.

t_ch: 这就是亡魂所说的炼金法阵——看来，需要分别置入「艾格勒的天空」、「吉奥里亚的大地」、「刻法勒的火焰」、「法吉娜的海洋」……
t_en: This must be the alchemical matrix the spirit mentioned. Let's see... We need to insert "Aquila's Sky," "Georios's Earth," "Kephale's Fire," "Phagousa's Ocean"...

t_ch: 还有，「墨涅塔的爱」……
t_en: And "Mnestia's Love"...

t_ch: 嘻，看来我们可爱的蜗居公主已经下定决心了……
t_en: It seems our sweet Princess Homebody has made up her mind...

t_ch: 既然这样，就不帮你专门带话了，裁缝女——我的时间可是非常宝贵的哪。
t_en: In that case, Seamstress, I suppose I no longer need to deliver your message — my time is rather precious, after all.

t_ch: 无论你是否意欲摆脱死亡，请遵从本心。以你曾经的诺言为证，我最后一次，请求你运使自己的使命——
t_en: Whether you wish to break free from death or not, please follow your heart. By the promise you once made, I ask that you fulfill your duty one final time —

t_ch: 遐蝶，请如人那般生…如人那般死。
t_en: Castorice, please live as a human... And die as one.

t_ch: 依照那位炼金术士的话语，最后的材料应当就是我自己……
t_en: According to that alchemist, the final piece is supposed to be me...

t_ch: 不过，他未曾如这般窥见往昔，未知全貌。
t_en: However, he is not privy to the past and therefore cannot see the full picture.

t_ch: …往昔？
t_en: ...The past?

t_ch: 于{RUBY_B#负世之泰坦}刻法勒{RUBY_E#}的回忆之中，我见过了「死亡」的双子。现在，我已能确信，我曾是其中之一。
t_en: I saw the twins of "Death" in {RUBY_B#Worldbearing Titan}Kephale{RUBY_E#}'s memory, and now I am certain that I was one of them.

t_ch: 「{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}灰黯之手{RUBY_E#}」的试炼，便是「夺去至亲的生命」。我在那时已然死去，倒在了新世界的门关前…而我那素未谋面的姊妹，成为了此世的泰坦{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}。
t_en: The trial of the "{RUBY_B#Death Titan}Hand of Shadow{RUBY_E#}" demanded "taking the life of your family." I died during that trial, falling before the gate to the new world... And my sister, whom I never met, became the Titan {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#} of this cycle.

t_ch: 对于「我」的死亡，她追悔莫及。而后，她以泰坦的神权将我的灵魂重塑，却也让自己从未在人前示现，无法被人寻获……
t_en: She was consumed with regret over my death, so she used the Titan's authority to reforge my soul. Yet, it also means she has never revealed herself to anyone, hidden away from the entire world...

t_ch: 因为…它一开始就为我而身陨，如今正静静地沉睡于冥河之中。
t_en: That's because the Titan she became already fell at the start of everything... They died for me and now lie in slumber in the River of Souls.

t_ch: 你是说…玻吕刻斯？
t_en: You mean... Pollux?

t_ch: 没错，冥河并非凡物，绝不会被轻易阻断。那拒绝死者往生者，惟有神性的残躯。
t_en: That's right. The River of Souls is no material river. It cannot be blocked by ordinary means. Only a divine corpse can prevent the dead from reaching the next world.

t_ch: 也正因此，要想取出「死亡」的火种，我们唯有使玻吕刻斯从死亡中苏醒……
t_en: And that's why we have to awaken Pollux from death before we can retrieve the Coreflame of "Death"...

t_ch: 利用「炼金」之法的力量，令它的残躯回归一身。
t_en: We have to reforge its body with the power of "alchemy."

t_ch: 那，按照刚才炼金术士的说法…小蝶你也是巨龙残躯的一部分？
t_en: But Cas, if that alchemist speaks the truth... does that mean you're also part of the dragon body?

t_ch: 在亡魂们的口中，我确实与那巨龙相伴而生……
t_en: According to those departed spirits, I was indeed born as the dragon's twin...

t_ch: 不过，在此之前，各位曾听到过声声叹息吗？
t_en: But before we talk about that... Have you heard the sighs?

t_ch: 回收灵魂的时候听到过…
t_en: I did when I was collecting the souls...

t_ch: 你一路上好像叹过很多次气。
t_en: You sighed many times on the way here...

t_ch: 不，并非是我，而是在回收灵魂时，于记忆之中传来的声音。
t_en: No, not from me. The sounds that come from the memories when we were collecting the souls.

t_ch: 啊，人家也听到了！所以那是？
t_en: Ah, I heard them too! So, what are those?

t_ch: 我想…那或许正是巨龙玻吕刻斯的遗愿实现的征兆吧。
t_en: I think... it's probably the sign of Pollux's wish being fulfilled.

t_ch: 在行使神权，复活死者时，它也一定意识到了那代价究竟有多沉重。因此，它用尽最后一丝余力，将我送到了无人敌视「死亡」的城邦——呼唤「死亡」的斯缇科西亚。
t_en: They must have realized how heavy a price they'd need to pay when they used their divine authority to resurrect a dead woman. That's why they exerted every ounce of their strength to send me to Styxia, a city that called for "Death" — A land where none is hostile to "Death."

t_ch: 为了让我过上新的人生，它却没能顺遂心愿，早早陨落…玻吕刻斯该有多想亲眼见证，它竭尽全力拯救的姐姐，今后生活得怎么样啊。
t_en: They never saw their wish fulfilled and fell so prematurely, all because they wanted to give me a new life... Pollux must really want to know how their sister has been after giving their all to save her.

t_ch: 也正是带着这样的心愿，即便身陨，玻吕刻斯的灵魂仍旧盘桓人间，静静地注视并记录着有关至亲的一切……
t_en: That wish is the reason Pollux's soul still lingers in the mortal realm even after their fall, quietly watching and documenting every detail of their sister's life...

t_ch: 啊！所以这一路上，我们不是捡拾到了某物……
t_en: Ah! So, instead of picking up objects on the way here...

t_ch: …而是给飘荡在此处的一缕思念，展现了某物。而我们将要把这些思念逐一编织，复原成玻吕刻斯完整的灵魂。
t_en: ...We were instead showing things to a strand of yearning that lingers here. Now we must weave these yearnings together and restore them into Pollux's complete soul.

t_ch: 可这些…都只是猜想吧？万一我们完成了仪式，结果却又完全不是这么一回事呢？
t_en: But all of this is just a hypothesis, right? What if we go through with the ritual, and it turns out totally different?

t_ch: 那也无妨。那刻夏老师总说「炼金」一向如此：我们能得到什么，只有在炼成的那一刻才能知晓。因此，这门学问才能被称为「造物的奇迹」。
t_en: That's fine. As Professor Anaxa often reminds us, such is the nature of alchemy. The true outcome can only be known once the process is complete. This is why alchemy is also known as the "Miracle of Creation."

t_ch: 现在，玻吕刻斯思念的拼图已甄于完整，只差最后一块碎片——
t_en: Now, the puzzle of Pollux's yearnings is nearly complete, with only one final piece remaining —

t_ch: 我将在炼金法阵中呈现，它无比想要知晓的一切：我离开斯缇科西亚后所经历的全部人生……
t_en: I shall reveal what they have been yearning to know in the alchemical matrix — I will show them my whole life since I left Styxia...

t_ch: …缇安大人已将它完整描绘，并交到我的手中了。
t_en: ...Lady Trianne has already drawn a picture of my entire life, and it is now in my hands.

t_ch: 「——至是，工程已毕，言尽于此。」
t_en: "...And now, the objective is complete. That is all."

t_ch: 那刻夏老师总喜欢在完成炼金准备后说上这样一句话。而现在——
t_en: Such is what Professor Anaxa always says after preparing for an alchemical ritual. And now —

t_ch: 玻吕刻斯，我会将你从冥河中抽离，然后重塑。或许你将因此受到惊扰，身形残缺可怖……
t_en: Pollux, I shall lift you from the River of Souls and reforge your soul. Perhaps you will be disturbed by this, and your body may become horrifying to behold...

t_ch: 但若是当初你引渡我的意识仍有一息尚存——请看向我吧。我想当面和你诉说感谢：你以神性为我换回的一切，我已经品尝过了。
t_en: But if even the slightest trace of your soul remains, the soul that once ferried me to this side of the shore, then please look at me. I wish to thank you in person — I have obtained everything you sacrificed your divinity for.

t_ch: 阿蒙内特阁下……
t_en: Lady Amunet...

t_ch: 她…认出你了？这应该是我们第一次见到能沟通的…灵魂吧？
t_en: Did she recognize you...? She must be the first soul we can communicate with here, right?

t_ch: 恐怕…她也和其他亡魂一样，仅仅是受困于此。只不过，她的片刻，我原本在场。
t_en: I'm afraid she is just another spirit trapped here, like the rest of them. The only difference is that I was there when she died.

t_ch: …请您继续吧，我在听。您要讲的，莫非是斯缇科西亚的故事？
t_en: ...Please, go on. I'm listening. Are you about to tell the story of Styxia?

t_ch: 正是，你果然如我想象的那样聪慧，但……
t_en: Yes. You're just as smart as I imagined, but...

t_ch: …罢了，为时尚早。
t_en: ...Nevermind. Now's not the time.

t_ch: 不必担心，长老。您看周围…这里已是亡魂的归处。
t_en: There's no need to worry, Elder. Take a look around you... You're already at the resting place of the dead.

t_ch: 亡魂的世界？这怎会……
t_en: The world of the dead? But how...

t_ch: 我明白，您当初是为了保护那位与死亡相伴的女孩，遐蝶，才选择对这故事缄口不言……
t_en: I understand that you chose to remain silent about the story to protect Castorice, the girl who coexists with death...

t_ch: 但现在，您不必再藏于心间了。我可以告诉您，那女孩的人生，已被您尽力守护住了。
t_en: But you no longer need to hide that story. I can assure you that you have protected that girl's life well.

t_ch: …也罢，那我就此展开吧。
t_en: ...Well, in that case, let me begin.

t_ch: 斯缇科西亚，那在童话中遭受无妄之灾，被死亡肆虐的海洋明珠——在哀地里亚之前，它是第一个转向「死亡」信仰的城邦。
t_en: Styxia, the pearl of the ocean, plagued by unwarranted calamity and ravaged by death in the fairy tale — It was the first city-state to shift their faith towards "Death," even before Aidonia did.

t_ch: 在现在的你看来…这或许有些不可思议吧？
t_en: From where you stand now, I suppose that's a bit hard to believe, right?

t_ch: 没错…我难以想象，身处黄金世，享受着无尽寿数的人们…如何会醉心于死亡？
t_en: Indeed... I cannot imagine that those from the Era Chrysea, blessed with eternal life, could ever yearn for death.

t_ch: 呵…我也一直在思考同样的问题，孩子。但恐怕这个问题没有确切的答案。
t_en: Heh... I have pondered the same question, child. But I'm afraid there is no clear answer to it.

t_ch: 「征途之所以伟大，史诗之所以壮阔，是因为万物终有逝去之时」——曾有人如此为我解释，为何死亡是世间必需之物。
t_en: "Journeys and epics are only glorious and magnificent because all things eventually fade to dust." That's how someone once explained to me why death is necessary to the world.

t_ch: 但我无法将它视作最后的解答…难道一个没有征伐与史诗的世界便不再美好了吗？
t_en: But I still can't take that as the final answer... Would a world without journeys and epics be worse off?

t_ch: 呵…对你说出这话的人，我想他或许是一位将军？斗士？
t_en: Heh... I take it the one who spoke this line was a general? Or perhaps a warrior?

t_ch: 我倒是从他的话语里得到了些启发。想听听我的见解吗，遐蝶？
t_en: I, however, did find some inspiration in these words. Would you like to hear my thoughts, Castorice?

t_ch: 请告诉我吧，阿蒙内特阁下。虽然，我心中对如何处置「死亡」的神权和火种已有决意……
t_en: Please enlighten me, Lady Amunet. Though I have already made up my mind on how to handle the authority and Coreflame of "Death"...

t_ch: 但，或许你的话语…能真正为我指明前进的方向。
t_en: Your words might truly guide me toward the path forward.

t_ch: 你我并非战士或统帅，「征途」和「史诗」也无法引起我心灵的共鸣。
t_en: You and I are neither warriors nor generals. Therefore, journeys and epics cannot truly resonate with us.

t_ch: 但若将这语境稍加修改…如果…<color=#dbc291ff>「爱」</color>并非墨涅塔赐予凡人的天性…反而是死亡降给我们的赐福呢？
t_en: But what if I reframe it like this — what if <color=#dbc291ff>"love"</color> is not a trait bestowed upon mortals by Mnestia, but a blessing from death?
"Love"...

t_ch: 在生命无尽的时代，无人发明「珍惜」二字——因为世间一切美好皆能无限次地被收入眼帘；一切被建立起的联系都无需担忧破碎。
t_en: No one ever invented the word "cherish" in the era when life was eternal, for all the good things in the world could be revisited endlessly, and every bond formed carried no risk of being severed.

t_ch: 所谓的黄金世，便是一个既无美德也无罪恶的时代。人们并非活着…而仅仅是存在于世上，倦惰地行走、等待。
t_en: The so-called Era Chrysea was an age devoid of both virtue and sin. People did not truly live — they simply existed, going through the motions without passion or purpose, then waiting for something to happen.

t_ch: 也许，斯缇科西亚人选择拥抱死亡，并非是迷恋上了它阴森可怖的一面……
t_en: Perhaps the people of Styxia chose to embrace death not out of fascination with its cruel and fearsome side...

t_ch: <color=#dbc291ff>而是伴随有限的生命降临的冲动和欲念，令他们沉醉痴狂，无法自拔。</color>
t_en: <color=#dbc291ff>But instead became fixated and obsessed with the impulses and desires tied to a finite existence.</color>

t_ch: 所以…「死亡」并非冰冷、绝望之物，而是……
t_en: Therefore, "Death" is not a thing of fear and desperation, but...

t_ch: 使用秘技击败天谴先锋
t_en: Use your Technique to defeat Furiae Praetor

t_ch: 没错，遐蝶。它是我们会在临别时含泪的理由…也是最初点燃凡人热情的薪火。
t_en: That's right, Castorice. It is the reason we shed tears when parting ways... and also the original spark that ignited the passion of mortals.

t_ch: 你若打算剥夺世间的「死亡」…那就要做好准备，目睹这世界回归停滞和怠惰。
t_en: If you truly intend to deprive the world of "Death," then be prepared to witness it return to a state of stagnation.

t_ch: 我理解了，阿蒙内特阁下。
t_en: I understand, Lady Amunet.

t_ch: 有限的生命…塑造了我们热爱且熟知的一切。即便是「死亡」的泰坦，也无权再将它从人的身上剥离。
t_en: Our limited lifespan shapes all that we know and love... Not even the Death Titan has the right to take that away from humankind again.

t_ch: 「墨涅塔的爱」…黄金之茧最初的赐福，大概并不为人所理解。直到{RUBY_B#死亡之泰坦}塞纳托斯{RUBY_E#}降临后，人们才学会了解读这份美好的赠礼。
t_en: "Mnestia's Love" was the Chrysalis of Gold's first blessing, but it most likely wasn't understood by mankind. We only learned to interpret this wonderful gift after {RUBY_B#Death Titan}Thanatos{RUBY_E#}' arrival.

t_ch: 斯缇科西亚女王为我接生…你将我纳入哀地里亚…阿格莱雅邀我加入逐火之旅…假如世间没有死亡，这一切故事皆不会发生。
t_en: If the world was bereft of death, then the Queen of Styxia wouldn't have played midwife in delivering me into this world... You wouldn't have welcomed me into Aidonia... And Aglaea would never have invited me to join the Flame-Chase Journey. None of these stories would ever have happened.

t_ch: 而我…自然就从未活过，更谈不上幸福。
t_en: And I... Would never have lived, let alone found happiness.

t_ch: 感谢您，我的母亲……
t_en: Thank you, mother...

t_ch: …我将以这漫长人生中全部的<color=#dbc291ff>「爱」</color>，完成最后的奇迹。
t_en: I will use all the <color=#dbc291ff>"love"</color> I've experienced in this long life to complete the final miracle.
